pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Protecting marine Hope Spots

pageCaption.value: Rolex has joined forces with Sylvia Earle’s Mission Blue initiative to watch over and safeguard the Earth’s oceans. Since 2009, Mission Blue has been creating a network of Hope Spots around the planet, flourishing icons of marine life in the immensity of the Earth’s Blue Heart.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Protecting marine Hope Spots

metadescription.value: Through its Perpetual Planet Initiative, Rolex supports Sylvia Earle's Mission Blue to protect vital marine ecosystems worldwide. More on rolex.org.

text.value:
The oceans are the source of life on Earth, yet despite their vastness they are in trouble. Legendary marine explorer Sylvia Earle has a powerful solution. She founded Mission Blue, a Perpetual Planet Initiative partner, and ignites change through a global network of “Hope Spots”, marine-protected areas of critical vulnerability.

Mission Blue works through local communities and assists them to make the case for marine protection. The organization also promotes understanding of the real value of the living oceans on which we depend: from coral reefs to fish, seagrass and mangroves, all are vital to maintain the integrity of the seas and the planet itself.

title.value:
No ocean; no life.
No ocean; no us.

Nusa Penida Hope Spot: Episode 1
Protecting the coral triangle

description.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are working closely with communities in Bali to protect some of the world’s most biodiverse coral reefs. Djohani founded the Coral Triangle Center (CTC) to manage and protect the marine environment and ensure sustainable livelihoods for villagers. Building on Balinese cultural traditions, the CTC has helped change attitudes to marine conservation. The organization contributed to the establishment of a Marine Protected Area (MPA) around the island of Nusa Penida, off the south coast of Bali, and in 2020, it was added to Mission Blue’s global network of Hope Spots.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Nusa Penida Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champion Rili Djohani has dedicated decades to protecting the vibrant marine world around her Balinese home. With support from the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative, she is helping local people to restore the coral reefs they depend on.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Nusa Penida Hope Spot

metadescription.value: Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are protecting and restoring marine habitats around the Balinese island of Nusa Penida. More on rolex.org.

title.value:
Nusa Penida Hope Spot: Episode 2
The ocean advocates

description.value: The Nusa Penida Marine Protected Area has helped conserve more than 20,000 hectares of coral reefs, mangroves and seagrass meadows off the south coast of Bali, Indonesia. Building on this success, Mission Blue Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are working to ensure the long-term resilience of these biodiverse marine ecosystems. They aim to inspire younger generations to become guardians of the ocean. Through Mission Blue’s global conservation network, they hope to make Nusa Penida a blueprint for marine conservation worldwide.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Nusa Penida Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champion Rili Djohani has dedicated decades to protecting the vibrant marine world around her Balinese home. With support from the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative, she is helping local people to restore the coral reefs they depend on.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Nusa Penida Hope Spot

metadescription.value: Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are protecting and restoring marine habitats around the Balinese island of Nusa Penida. More on rolex.org.

description.value: Founded in 2008, Marine Conservation Cambodia has been installing artificial reefs to restore the depleted seabed around the Kep Archipelago since 2016. The programme, supported by our Perpetual Planet Initiative partner Mission Blue, has seen more than 1,000 hectares of seagrass ecosystems restored around the islands. The return of seagrass has led to wildlife thriving in the area, including the endangered Irrawaddy dolphin and green turtle. The ocean’s largest vegetarian, the dugong, has also returned to Cambodian waters, years after it was last seen there.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Kep Archipelago Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Conservationists are working with local communities to repair degraded marine habitats in Cambodia. With support from the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative, they are restoring seagrass meadows to bring the ocean’s largest vegetarian back to Cambodia’s waters.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Kep Archipelago Hope Spot

metadescription.value: Conservationists in Cambodia are working to revive the Kep Archipelago's seagrass meadows and, in turn, bring back threatened marine life. More on rolex.org.

title.value:
Kep Archipelago Hope Spot: Episode 2
Connected coast

description.value: Since 2016, with the help of local communities, Marine Conservation Cambodia has installed more than 300 artificial reefs in Mission Blue’s Kep Archipelago Hope Spot. The result has been the regrowth of some 1,000 hectares of seagrass, and increased sightings of endangered wildlife. Thanks to the overwhelming success of the project, the organization has been contracted by the Cambodian government to deploy artificial reefs along the country’s coastline. It will install 5,000 artificial reefs over the next five-and-a-half years.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Kep Archipelago Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Conservationists are working with local communities to repair degraded marine habitats in Cambodia. With support from the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative, they are restoring seagrass meadows to bring the ocean’s largest vegetarian back to Cambodia’s waters.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Kep Archipelago Hope Spot

metadescription.value: Conservationists in Cambodia are working to revive the Kep Archipelago's seagrass meadows and, in turn, bring back threatened marine life. More on rolex.org.

title.value:
Exmouth Gulf and Ningaloo Coast Hope Spot
Uncharted waters

description.value: Beneath the turquoise waters of Australia’s World Heritage-listed Ningaloo Coast is one of the planet’s largest fringing coral reefs. Although highly protected and recognized for its biodiversity, many of its animals depend on a less-protected area on the other side of the cape: Exmouth Gulf. Mission Blue Hope Spot Champion Ben Fitzpatrick has led his team on an expedition to the gulf’s unexplored waters. Using the latest technology in drones and underwater photography, they are surveying its incredibly biodiverse wildlife and proving how important the area is to the region and to the nearby Ningaloo Reef.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Exmouth Gulf and Ningaloo Coast Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Australia’s Ningaloo Coast teems with marine life, but its survival depends on the biodiverse Exmouth Gulf nearby.

WaPageTitle.value: Uncharted Waters

metadescription.value: Hope Spot Champion Ben Fitzpatrick is working to protect Australia's Exmouth Gulf and demonstrate its crucial links to Ningaloo Coast. More on rolex.org.

title.value:
Malpelo Island Hope Spot: Episode 1
An ocean oasis

description.value: The waters around Malpelo Island, situated hundreds of kilometres from the coast of Colombia, are home to breathtaking biodiversity. Whale sharks, groupers and hawksbill turtles swim below the surface, with rare species of bird swooping above. As a Mission Blue Hope Spot Champion, Sandra Bessudo runs research expeditions to the seamount twice a year. After sailing for 36 hours to get there, Bessudo and scientists study the ecosystems and species populations. What they learn helps to conserve the island’s marine environment and expand protection in the region.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Malpelo Island Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Sandra Bessudo has dedicated her life to the wild waters of Malpelo Island. Today, with the support of the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative and Mission Blue, Bessudo is working tirelessly to help improve and expand the marine protection around Malpelo to preserve its breathtaking biodiversity.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Malpelo Island Hope Spot

metadescription.value: Hope Spot Champion Sandra Bessudo is working to help expand marine protection around Malpelo Island to preserve its biodiversity. More on rolex.org.

title.value:
Malpelo Island Hope Spot: Episode 2
Conservation in numbers

description.value: Naturalist and professional diver Sandra Bessudo has worked for decades to conserve marine life around Malpelo Island, off the coast of Colombia. The island, a Mission Blue Hope Spot, is home to important ecosystems that support the wider health of the South Pacific Ocean. Together with neighbouring Hope Spot Champions, Bessudo works to create additional “swimways” between Malpelo and other regional protected areas that help increase the populations of migrating species such as hammerhead and silky sharks.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Malpelo Island Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Sandra Bessudo has dedicated her life to the wild waters of Malpelo Island. Today, with the support of the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative and Mission Blue, Bessudo is working tirelessly to help improve and expand the marine protection around Malpelo to preserve its breathtaking biodiversity.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Malpelo Island Hope Spot

metadescription.value: Hope Spot Champion Sandra Bessudo is working to help expand marine protection around Malpelo Island to preserve its biodiversity. More on rolex.org.

title.value:
Galápagos Expedition: Episode 1
A blueprint for the planet

description.value: “Think like an ocean”. Working to protect the iconic Galápagos Islands Marine Reserve, Sylvia Earle and her Mission Blue team led a groundbreaking expedition to assess the health of the ocean after nearly 25 years of protection. What they found could be used as a blueprint of success for the rest of the planet.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Galápagos Islands Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Through its Perpetual Planet Initiative, Rolex is supporting ocean conservation non-profit Mission Blue in its goal to create a global network of Hope Spots, areas that deserve protection as they are home to vital marine ecosystems.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Galápagos Islands Hope Spot

metadescription.value: The waters of the Galápagos Islands, named a Hope Spot by Mission Blue, are a unique living laboratory of diverse endemic species. More on rolex.org.

description.value: Mission Blue has been helping to track animals in the Galápagos Marine Reserve to analyse their well-being around the archipelago. Through this work, legendary oceanographer  Sylvia Earle and a team of scientists are making incredible insights into the connectivity of the Galápagos with the rest of the planet – including discovering DNA sequences that could be completely new to science.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Galápagos Islands Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Through its Perpetual Planet Initiative, Rolex is supporting ocean conservation non-profit Mission Blue in its goal to create a global network of Hope Spots, areas that deserve protection as they are home to vital marine ecosystems.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Galápagos Islands Hope Spot

metadescription.value: The waters of the Galápagos Islands, named a Hope Spot by Mission Blue, are a unique living laboratory of diverse endemic species. More on rolex.org.

description.value: In the Galápagos Islands, Manuel Yépez used to catch and sell sharks. Today, he is one of two Mission Blue “Champions” in the archipelago; logging, sampling and distributing information about the diverse marine life he comes across. Through combined efforts with local fishermen like him, Sylvia Earle and Mission Blue are fighting to protect the incredible diversity of the Galápagos Islands, passing the torch to the next generation in the process.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Galápagos Islands Hope Spot

bloc2Text.value: Hope Spots are scattered across the oceans – from Antarctic seas to atolls in the Pacific. On the globe, each pin represents a sanctuary vital to the preservation of marine species. Anyone can nominate an area and the application assessed by Mission Blue’s Hope Spot Council. Every year, more Hope Spots are added, lighting up the world with the power of public support for ocean conservation.

description.value: The only sea without a land boundary, it occupies almost two thirds of the North Atlantic and is bounded only by a whirling gyre of four powerful currents that rotate in a clockwise direction giving rise to a large patch of ocean that is inordinately clear, warm and still.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Osa Peninsula Hope Spot

pageCaption.value: Emerald rainforests, turquoise waters lapping on to secluded beaches, blissful snorkelling and scuba diving. It is easy to sell the allure of Costa Rica’s Osa Peninsula to tourists in search of an adventure holiday: National Geographic once described the area as “the most biologically intense place on Earth”.

WaPageTitle.value: Cathedral in the sea

metadescription.value: Osa Peninsula, an important Costa Rican route for marine life, has been named a Hope Spot by Sylvia Earle's Mission Blue. Discover more on rolex.org.

description.value: The seas off Osa Peninsula form part of a migratory route for ocean giants such as humpback whales, whale sharks, manta rays and hammerhead sharks that breed in its warm waters. At migration time, the visitors follow an underwater mountain chain to the Galápagos or Cocos islands.

place.value: Off the coast of Costa Rica
Gulf of Panama, approximately 48 kilometers off its Pacific Coast

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue -Watching over the Earth’s Blue Heart

pageCaption.value: Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative supports those using science to understand the world’s environmental challenges and who are finding ways to restore balance to our ecosystems, particularly the oceans, which are under siege from a host of pressures.

WaPageTitle.value: Watching over the Earth’s Blue Heart

metadescription.value: Rolex has partnered with Sylvia Earle’s Mission Blue initiative to establish a network of Marine Protected Areas around the globe. More on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Watching over the Earth’s Blue Heart

description.value: Discover the new generation of heroes who are showing us what needs to be done to restore health to the ocean, creating a Perpetual Planet for us all.

title.value: The mighty Zambezi River and its secrets
The 2019 Rolex National Geographic Explorers of the Year - The Okavango Wilderness Project Team
Fernando Trujillo: For the love of river dolphins

$AppRoute:97C7D783CFAF4429AAE0C1AAD3CC25.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champion Rili Djohani has dedicated decades to protecting the vibrant marine world around her Balinese home. With support from the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative, she is helping local people to restore the coral reefs they depend on.

$AppRoute:69B7449A1E2C45119092413341FD65.value: Conservationists are working with local communities to repair degraded marine habitats in Cambodia. With support from the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative, they are restoring seagrass meadows to bring the ocean’s largest vegetarian back to Cambodia’s waters.

$AppRoute:0A92DB9032D249A7B66B901B8A8D6A.value: Australia’s Ningaloo Coast teems with marine life, but its survival depends on the biodiverse Exmouth Gulf nearby.

$AppRoute:E04FE730EAD742A7AEFADF014DCC89.value: Sandra Bessudo has dedicated her life to the wild waters of Malpelo Island. Today, with the support of the Rolex Perpetual Planet Initiative and Mission Blue, Bessudo is working tirelessly to help improve and expand the marine protection around Malpelo to preserve its breathtaking biodiversity.

$AppRoute:1D1E192C055743AAAE90CC18359FE2.value: Through its Perpetual Planet Initiative, Rolex is supporting ocean conservation non-profit Mission Blue in its goal to create a global network of Hope Spots, areas that deserve protection as they are home to vital marine ecosystems.

$AppRoute:7DE3347C0F904F8F9076A12B624F0C.value: Perpetual Planet initiative partner Mission Blue is throwing the spotlight on protection of the fabled Sargasso Sea with the arrival of the Sargasso Sea Commission as its formal Champion.

$AppRoute:45AAE81195104C03A74B396B165829.value: Emerald rainforests, turquoise waters lapping on to secluded beaches, blissful snorkelling and scuba diving. It is easy to sell the allure of Costa Rica’s Osa Peninsula to tourists in search of an adventure holiday: National Geographic once described the area as “the most biologically intense place on Earth”.

$AppRoute:6FCBB109B9594A63B6D98685FC32DF.value: Through its Perpetual Planet initiative, Rolex is supporting ocean conservation non-profit Mission Blue in its goal to create a global network of Hope Spots, areas that deserve protection as they are home to vital marine ecosystems.
A whale cafe in the Azores

$AppRoute:D41CD79DA3EB4A8AB13B3F3B3ECE4E.value: Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative supports those using science to understand the world’s environmental challenges and who are finding ways to restore balance to our ecosystems, particularly the oceans, which are under siege from a host of pressures.
Watching over the Earth’s Blue Heart

$AppRoute:0A9BA5768FF0419E952291869579CC.value: Rolex has joined forces with Sylvia Earle’s Mission Blue initiative to watch over and safeguard the Earth’s oceans. Since 2009, Mission Blue has been creating a network of Hope Spots around the planet, flourishing icons of marine life in the immensity of the Earth’s Blue Heart.
